name: IEX Cloud API
what_it_does: Real-time and historical stock market data API providing quotes, financials, earnings, news, crypto, and forex data via REST and SSE streaming.
use_cases: Real-time stock quote retrieval for portfolio monitoring agents
Fundamental data analysis (earnings, financials, balance sheets)
News and sentiment aggregation for trading signal agents
Historical OHLCV data for backtesting pipelines
not_for: Ultra-low-latency trading requiring sub-millisecond data feeds
Options chain data or derivatives analytics
best_when: An agent needs consistent, well-structured US equity data with real-time quotes and fundamental financials from a single endpoint.
avoid_when: You need tick-by-tick Level 2 order book data or are building high-frequency trading infrastructure.

auth.notes: Single API token passed as query parameter (token=) or Authorization header. Separate publishable vs secret tokens for client vs server use. No fine-grained scope control.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 50,000 core messages/month (sufficient for basic dev use)
pricing.notes: Credit-based system where each endpoint costs a different number of messages. Real-time data and premium datasets cost more credits. Free tier has no credit card requirement.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by plan; free tier throttled, paid plans higher limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: HTTP status codes used consistently; JSON error bodies with message field. Credit exhaustion errors are clear but per-endpoint credit costs require consulting a separate table.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All endpoints are read-only GET requests; inherently idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Credit consumption is endpoint-specific and not obvious at call time; agents can exhaust credits unexpectedly
API token passed as query parameter is logged in server access logs by default
SSE streaming endpoint requires persistent connection management not native to simple HTTP clients
Some endpoints return empty arrays vs null for missing data inconsistently
Free tier message limits reset monthly, not rolling 30 days

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. However, API key as query parameter is a security anti-pattern that leaks tokens into server logs and browser history. No scope granularity on free/mid tiers. Publishable token concept helps client-side use cases.
